reasoning: IPG is in the final stages of a stock-for-stock acquisition by Omnicom (0.344 OMC shares per IPG share), expected to close in Q4 2025. IPG's stock price will function primarily as a tracking stock for OMC's price. Over any given 1-week period, a mature stock like OMC (and by extension IPG) has approximately a 51-54% chance of increasing in value. IPG will file its Q3 10-Q on Nov 10, but has canceled its earnings call, indicating muted idiosyncratic volatility. Absent clear catalysts that would drive OMC/IPG uniformly higher or lower in the Nov 9 - Nov 16 window (such as a scheduled dividend ex-date or OMC earnings, which typically occur in October), the probability of the stock closing strictly higher stays close to the historical base rate for weekly equity returns of around 52%.
